globalPolicies.detectorIndependence: Each mechanism is trained, gated and reported independently before any composition.
globalPolicies.outerOosPolicy: Feature values, labels and outcomes from outer OOS are forbidden for dossier adjudication, fitting and threshold selection.
globalPolicies.missingnessPolicy: Missing, unavailable and not-applicable methodology regimes remain explicit; zero imputation and cross-regime silent splicing are forbidden.
globalPolicies.thresholdPolicy: No threshold is frozen in E14.4. Future thresholds may be selected only inside inner leave-one-episode-out folds.

positiveRequirements: one official narrative identifying disruption in the named mechanism
one quantitative observation corroborating timing and direction
month boundaries justified independently of model predictions
hardNegativeRequirements: one official narrative affirmatively describing orderly functioning or contained transmission in the named mechanism
one quantitative observation showing the named mechanism remained within its documented historical regime
absence of labels, interventions or alerts is never evidence
dossierRules.requiredFollowupGate: A separate label-foundation audit must bind accepted dossiers before ground truth can change.

phaseModel.onsetDefinition: First month crossing a future inner-fitted entry threshold from calm.
phaseModel.activeDefinition: Months after onset while the independent mechanism remains above its future exit threshold.
phaseModel.recoveryDefinition: First of two consecutive months below a future exit threshold, reported separately from onset quality.

detectors.target: Broad, cross-asset risk repricing and impaired market risk-bearing.
detectors.positiveDefinition: Officially documented broad repricing or market-liquidity disruption corroborated by volatility, drawdown or credit-risk observations.
detectors.hardNegativeDefinition: A material non-target shock during which official evidence and market observations affirm that broad risk-bearing and market functioning remained orderly.

detectors.target: Disruption in unsecured or secured short-term funding and market liquidity.
detectors.positiveDefinition: Officially documented funding disruption corroborated by a spread or liquidity observation valid in the applicable reference-rate regime.
detectors.hardNegativeDefinition: A material shock during which official evidence affirmatively reports orderly short-term funding and the applicable spread remains within its documented historical regime.

detectors.target: Banking-system stress or impaired credit intermediation.
detectors.positiveDefinition: Officially documented banking or credit-intermediation stress corroborated by release-dated bank financials or credit spreads.
detectors.hardNegativeDefinition: An institution-specific failure or macro shock for which official evidence affirmatively shows containment and stable system-level intermediation.

detectors.target: External growth or emerging-market shock transmitted into U.S.-relevant financial conditions.
detectors.positiveDefinition: Officially documented cross-border transmission corroborated by exchange-rate or U.S. risk-spread observations.
detectors.hardNegativeDefinition: A foreign or domestic shock for which official evidence affirmatively shows contained transmission and no material U.S.-relevant cross-border financial disruption.
